subject: Biology
title: Fungal Diseases
description: A quick, practical guide to Fungal Diseases.

hook.text: Fungi cause infections ranging from mild athlete's foot to life-threatening lung disease.

buildup.text: Candida yeast lives harmlessly in your body but causes thrush when the immune system weakens.

discovery.text: Aspergillus mold spores float in the air. Healthy lungs clear them, but weakened lungs can't.

twist.text: Fungal infections kill about 1.5 million people a year—more than malaria.

climax.text: Antifungal drugs are limited, and resistant fungi like Candida auris are spreading in hospitals.

punchline.text: Fungi are quiet killers hiding in plain air.

quiz.question: How many people do fungal infections kill each year?
